text:
Gidal (ジダル, Jidaru) is the Private Guard of the Second Prince Camilla in charge of killing the Fifth Prince Tubeppa through a Nen curse. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

Appearance

Gidal has short, dark hair, a prominent forehead looming over their small eyes, long eyelashes, and a large, square jaw. They wears the standard uniform of Camilla's private guards. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

After Camilla is confined to a room in the VVIP area, Gidal and most of her other bodyguards attend a meeting called by Sarahell about Shikaku's suicide. 

Abilities & Powers

As a Provisional Hunter, Gidal has all the benefits granted by their status as a Hunter. However, these are all temporary, and their Hunter status and license will expire after the voyage to the New Continent. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 

Nen

Gidal is a Nen user and can cast the same curse as the other Have-Nots in Prince Camilla's employ. 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

The furigana name of the Have-Nots' curse is derived from Japanese mythology, specifically to Izanami's inability to leave the underworld due to having eaten of its food.
The kanji name of the curse, "Tsujitsuma Awase ni Umareta Bokura", is derived from a song by Amazarashi.
Viz localized the curse's name as "Dust in the Wind: Hell Fruit".
